---
title: "Sample processing"
description: "Per-sample QC and filtering, then cohort normalization and PCA."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 30
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

Sample processing takes the per-sample [ingested](ingest-10x.qmd) `.h5mu` files
and produces one clean, normalized cohort object with a PCA embedding, ready for
[integration](integrate.qmd). `process_samples` wraps the whole thing, per-sample
QC and filtering, concatenation, normalization, highly-variable-feature
selection, and PCA, into a single command.

## Options

| Workflow / component | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`multiomics/process_samples`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/multiomics/process_samples.qmd) | The standard entry point: per-sample QC + cohort processing in one run |
| [`correction/cellbender_remove_background`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/correction/cellbender_remove_background.qmd) | Optional first step: remove ambient RNA from raw counts before QC |

## Example: process a cohort

For example, pass a [`param_list`](process-many-samples.qmd) of ingested samples
with your QC thresholds:

```yaml
# params.yaml
param_list:
  - id: sample_A
    input: results/ingestion/sample_A.h5mu
  - id: sample_B
    input: results/ingestion/sample_B.h5mu
rna_min_counts: 500
rna_max_counts: 50000
rna_min_genes_per_cell: 200
max_fraction_mito: 0.2
publish_dir: results/processed
```

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/multiomics/process_samples/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

## Key choices

**What QC removes.** The raw matrix holds more than real cells:

- **Empty droplets**, barcodes that caught ambient RNA (very low counts).
- **Dying / damaged cells**, low counts with a high mitochondrial fraction (mito
  RNA leaks into the cytoplasm when the membrane is damaged).
- **Doublets**, two cells sequenced as one, at roughly double the expected depth.

**Thresholds are dataset-specific.** Inspect the distributions first with a
[QC report](qc-report.qmd), then set `rna_min_counts` / `rna_max_counts` /
`rna_min_genes_per_cell` / `max_fraction_mito` at natural breakpoints.

**Ambient RNA.** For heavily contaminated data, run
`cellbender_remove_background` on the raw counts before QC.

**CITE-seq.** Protein (ADT) counts are normalized with CLR alongside the RNA
steps automatically.

## Output

One merged `.h5mu`: normalized counts in `.layers["log_normalized"]`, selected
features flagged in `.var`, a PCA embedding in `.obsm["X_pca"]`, and
`.obs["sample_id"]`. Next: [integration](integrate.qmd).

:::{.callout-tip}
Why per-sample steps (QC, filtering) run before concatenation and cohort steps
(normalization, PCA) run after: see
[Advanced architecture](../concepts/data-state.qmd).
:::
